title: Lower Body Power Block
desc: Heavy lower body session combining compound lifts with plyometric work. Priority on deadlift → squat → mobility-strength compounds.

ex.note: Get blood flowing
Only when fully recovered

title: Upper Body Strength
desc: Comprehensive upper body session with eccentric shoulder health accessories. Heavy pressing and pulling patterns.

ex.note: Get blood flowing
4 sec eccentric
4 sec eccentric
4 sec eccentric

title: Wave Loading: 3/2/1
desc: Wave loading for peak strength. Two waves of 3/2/1 on deadlift and bench. Increase weight within each wave and start wave 2 heavier than wave 1. Goal: +2.5-5 kg per week.

ex.note: Increase intensity over 5 min
Practise hinge, lock lats
Explosive off the floor. Brace hard.
Heavier — stay tight.
Max effort single.
Start heavier than wave 1 triple.
Load hamstrings and glutes. Soft knees.
Explosive hip extension drives the bar.

title: Zercher Lunges + Weighted Chins
desc: Ascending strength block — increase load every round, finishing heavier than you started. Finishes with high-volume erg intervals.

ex.note: Get blood flowing
Essential for Zercher lunge depth
Own your depth — chest up
Upper back prep for chin-up load
Open the shoulder girdle
Round 1 — find position. Bar sits in elbow crooks, elbows high.
Round 2 — increase weight.
Round 3 — challenging.
Round 4 — heaviest. Slow and controlled.
10-15s per 500m faster than your time trial pace. Consistency across all sets — don't start too hard.
On pumped legs — embrace it.

title: Hamstring & Posterior Chain
desc: Posterior chain specialisation. Sumo for hip loading, RDLs for length, Nordics for eccentric strength, hip thrust for glute power.

ex.note: Squeeze hard at top — drive knees out
Walk hands out, pike up, walk back. Feel the stretch.
Prep thoracic and lumbar spine
Set hips low, knees out, drive the floor away. Don't jerk.
3s eccentric. Feel the hamstring stretch at the bottom. Spine stays neutral throughout.
Full glute extension at top. Don't hyperextend the lower back.
3-4s eccentric descent. Explosive pull back up. Partner anchors feet.
2s squeeze at peak contraction.
Hinge — keep back flat. Glute-driven.
Full ROM — stretch at the bottom.

title: Pull Day — Back & Biceps
desc: Back-dominant pull day. Lat width first, upper back thickness second, bicep volume last. Prioritise weight on row and weighted pull-ups.

ex.note: Build tempo over 5 min
Squeeze shoulder blades together at finish
Full passive hang — opens the shoulder girdle
No arm bend — depress and retract scapulae only
Prep thoracic spine
Chest up. Row to belly button. Control the eccentric.
Full hang at bottom. Chin over bar at top. No kipping.
Keep chest tall — don't lean back excessively.
Pull to upper chest. Keep elbows close.
Elbows high and wide. Pull to nose level. Essential for shoulder health.
Supinate hard at the top. No swinging.
Neutral grip — targets brachialis.
Fully stretched position — maximum bicep recruitment.

title: Push Day — Chest, Shoulders, Triceps
desc: Horizontal and vertical push patterns. Flat bench for pec strength, OHP for shoulder development, isolation to fill volume.

ex.note: Controlled descent, full ROM
Lock shoulder blades, feel the groove
Shoulder blades retracted. Drive feet into floor. Bar to lower pec.
Brace core tight. Bar travels in a straight vertical path.
30-45° incline. 2-3s eccentric. Upper pec focus.
Lead with elbow, not wrist. Slight forward lean.
Forward lean = more chest. Upright = more tricep.
Elbows in. Lower to forehead or just behind head.
Lock elbows at sides. Full extension at the bottom.

title: Overhead Strength Day
desc: Overhead pressing priority. Push press for power, strict press for raw strength, accessories for lateral development and shoulder health.

ex.note: Back flat against wall. Slide arms overhead. Maintain contact.
Rotator cuff activation
Explosive hip extension + full lockout overhead
Explosive dip-drive. Create maximum bar speed. Lock out overhead with active shoulders.
No leg drive. Brace full. Bar travels in straight vertical path.
Rotate palms-facing to palms-forward as you press.
Requires good mobility. Full range — control the descent.
Elbows high and wide. Rotator cuff health.
Wide grip. Elbows lead. Do not pull past chin.

title: Full Body 5×5
desc: Classic 5×5 alternating sessions. Add 2.5 kg to upper body lifts and 5 kg to deadlift each session. One of the most proven strength protocols.

ex.note: Find depth and position. Practise the walkout.
Full depth. Knees track toes. Brace hard before unracking.
Shoulders retracted. Bar to lower pec. Drive feet.
Row to belly — not chest. Control the eccentric.
Standing. Full lockout. No excessive back lean.
1×5 only — it is heavy enough. More sets become junk volume here.

title: Squat Intensive
desc: High-volume squat session. Work up to heavy triples, build positional strength with pause squats, then quad isolation volume.

ex.note: Own the depth — no caving knees
Practise bar position, brace, and walkout
Ramp warm-up
Work sets. Full depth every rep.
3-second pause in the hole. Builds positional strength and depth confidence.
Reduced spinal load — go heavy here.

title: Arms & Core Finisher
desc: Arms and core specialisation block. Standalone or finisher. Full range every rep — no ego, just contractions.

ex.note: Get blood into biceps
Controlled — warm up the tricep
Activate core before loading
Supinate hard at the top. No swinging.
Lock elbows at sides. Full extension.
Elbow on inner thigh. 1s hold at peak.
Long head emphasis. Keep elbows close.
7 bottom half + 7 top half + 7 full range. No stopping.
Squeeze everything. Hips level.
Keep hips level. Full extension.
Legs fully extended.
Opposite arm + leg. Lower back pressed to floor.

title: 30-Min Max Distance Test
desc: Aerobic capacity test. Run as far as possible in 30 minutes. Record distance and average HR. Aim for a negative split — second half faster than the first.

ex.note: Very easy — wake up the legs only
Build to 85% — fire the fast-twitch fibres
Start controlled at ~80% effort. Increase at the 15-min mark. Final 5 min: everything you have. Record total distance.
Lower HR gradually. Don't sit down immediately.

title: Negative Split Run
desc: 50-minute progressive run. Every 10 minutes the pace increases. Finishing faster than you started trains the race-specific ability to push when fatigued.

ex.note: Easy — conversational pace. ~9:00-9:30/mi
Moderate — breathing harder, still controlled. ~8:30/mi
Tempo — comfortably hard. ~8:00/mi
Race effort — focused, deliberate. ~7:30/mi
Above time trial pace — this is your race. 7:00/mi or faster

title: Long Aerobic Run
desc: Long slow distance. Below 70% HR max. Builds the aerobic engine. Optional last-mile sprint to simulate race-day kick.

ex.note: Below 70% HR max. If you can't hold a full conversation, slow down. Trust the process.
After 75+ min easy, push hard. Trains your race-day kick.

title: Speed Play Intervals (Fartlek)
desc: 1 min hard / 1 min easy. Hard at 5k race pace, easy at marathon pace. Trains ability to sustain speed and recover under fatigue.

ex.note: Gradually build pace toward threshold
Hard: 5k pace or faster. Easy: actual marathon pace — real recovery. Count rounds. Add 1 round each week.

title: 500m Row / Ski Intervals
desc: Lactate threshold erg intervals. Each piece at 10-15s faster per 500m than your time trial pace. Pacing consistency is the goal.

ex.note: Build intensity gradually. Low drag.
Start easy, finish at tempo. Not full effort.
10-15s faster per 500m than time trial pace. Don't start too hard and fade — consistency across all sets.
On pumped legs — a training stimulus in itself.

title: Tempo Run — Steady State
desc: Sustained effort at lactate threshold — comfortably hard. Builds your ability to hold race pace for extended periods.

ex.note: Gradually build pace toward threshold
75-85% HR max. ~6:15-7:00/mile. Comfortably hard — 3-4 words, not full sentences. Run by feel, not pace.

title: Hill Repeats
desc: Short explosive uphill efforts. Builds running power, quad and glute strength, and VO2max without flat-sprint joint impact.

ex.note: 70% effort — just feel the incline
90-95% effort going up. Drive knees, pump arms. Full recovery before next rep — hill repeats only work if you come back fresh.
Hills are tough on hip flexors

title: 400m Track Intervals
desc: Classic 400m repeats at 5k-3k race pace. One of the most effective VO2max sessions. Consistent pace across all reps is key.

ex.note: Build to 95% — get legs turning over fast
5k-3k race pace. If pace drops more than 3s per rep, stop — it has become junk volume. Consistency is everything.
Quad, hamstring, hip flexor, calf — 30s each

title: Easy Recovery Run + Strides
desc: Easy conversational run + short strides. Maintains neuromuscular sharpness without adding fatigue. Ideal the day after a hard session.

ex.note: Zone 1-2. Below 65% HR max. If you feel awful, cut to 20 min or walk. Recovery only works if you actually recover.
Build to 90% over 5s, hold briefly, coast down. Keeps legs sharp without adding training stress.

title: Zone 2 Bike Session
desc: Steady-state zone 2 cycling. Low impact aerobic base building. Great choice after heavy leg sessions when running would add too much stress.

ex.note: Smooth pedal stroke, wake up the legs
60-70% HR max. Hold a full conversation. If HR creeps up, reduce resistance. Base building — resist the urge to push.
Target 85-95 RPM. Higher cadence = less muscular fatigue, more cardiovascular stimulus.

title: Session 1/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 1 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility for Weightlifting. Focus: ankle, knee, and squat mechanics.

title: Session 2/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 1 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility for Weightlifting. Focus: neck, chest, wrists, and shoulder overhead position.

title: Session 3/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 1 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility for Weightlifting. Focus: calf, hip, inner thigh mobility.

title: Session 4/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 2 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: thoracic spine, lats, chest, and shoulder activation.

title: Session 5/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 2 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: wrist, chest, upper back, and front rack position.

title: Session 6/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 2 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: neck, chest, wrist, and scapular control.

title: Session 7/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 3 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: spine, lats, elbow, shoulder, and ankle for front rack.

title: Session 8/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 3 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: ankle, knee, hip flexor. Progresses to air overhead squat.

ex.n: Supported leg kick with hip rotation

title: Session 9/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 3 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: wrist, chest, arm abduction in tripod, lat band, and ankle.

ex.n: Rack elbows rotation + Front Rack hold

title: Session 10/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 4 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: calf, hip, inner thigh with kettlebell marches.

title: Lactate Buffer EMOM
desc: 16-min EMOM targeting lactate buffering. Hard but manageable — increase each round.

ex.note: Increase each round.

title: The Beast — 45-Min AMRAP
desc: Grueling 45-minute AMRAP. Go to war on the heavy carry.

ex.note: Go to war on this!

title: Ski School — 10-Min AMRAP
desc: 10-min AMRAP with ski erg and DB complex.

ex.note: Move hard and under control.

title: Conditioning Chipper
desc: Classic pacing chipper. Find your sweet spot and hold it.

ex.note: Pace yourself.

title: EMOmania
desc: Double 12-min alternating EMOM. Push rep count each round.

ex.note: Score: level achieved.

title: "Tristan" — 2:00 on / 1:00 off
desc: 4 rounds: 7 deadlifts + 14 cal ski + max burpees. 2 min work / 1 min rest.

ex.note: 2 min work / 1 min rest. Rounds 3-4: extend to 3 min.

title: "Fran" — CrossFit Classic
desc: The queen of benchmark WODs. 21-15-9 thrusters and pull-ups.

title: "Cindy" — 20-Min AMRAP
desc: Classic benchmark AMRAP. 5 pull-ups + 10 push-ups + 15 air squats.

title: "Helen" — 3 Rounds for Time
desc: 3 rounds: 400m run + 21 KB swings + 12 pull-ups.

title: Tabata Classic
desc: Tabata intervals across 4 stations. 8 rounds of 20s work / 10s rest per station.

title: C&J + Snatch Complex
desc: High-volume Olympic complexes. Every 3 min. Clean complex → snatch balance → snatch complex.

ex.n: Empty bar: Deadlift + Front squat + OH press + Snatch balance
Power clean + Squat clean + Hang power clean + Hang squat clean

title: C&J Hang Focus — % Work
desc: Percentage-based C&J with hang emphasis. Back squats after lifting.

ex.n: Empty bar: Front squat + Hang squat clean + Split jerk + Push press
Squat clean + Hang squat clean + Jerk
AMRAP 12 min: 14 DB snatches + 10 box jump overs + 6 burpee box jumps + 2 wall walks

title: Snatch — Slow Pull Focus
desc: Technically demanding. Slow pull structures the full squat snatch.

ex.n: Empty bar: Snatch RDL + High pull + Muscle snatch + OHS
Snatch lift off + Slow-pull squat snatch + Squat snatch
E4:00 × 4-5: Cal row + 10 power snatches + bar-facing burpees + 10 OHS

title: Push Press / Jerk + Deadlift
desc: Olympic jerk work paired with heavy deadlifts and long conditioning.

ex.n: AMRAP 18 min: 500m row + 50 DUs + 20 cal bike + 15 KB swings

title: Clean Pull + Snatch Pull
desc: Pulling strength session. Build the foundation of the clean and snatch with heavy pulls.

ex.n: Tall clean (from standing, no knee bend)

title: Pause Clean Session
desc: Pause work builds body awareness and strength in the bottom position.

title: Jerk Balance + BTN Press
desc: Jerk footwork and receiving position. Balance drills to find the correct landing.

title: Power Position Drills
desc: Work from the power position to perfect hip extension and turnover timing.

ex.n: Snatch-grip deadlift to power pos + shrug

title: Snatch from Blocks
desc: Blocks eliminate the first pull and isolate positions 2 and 3.

title: Clean Deadlift + Front Squat
desc: Strength foundation for the clean. Heavy pulls and front squats build the base.

title: Sled Day — Heavy
desc: Heavy sled work with runs between sets. Train strength and HR management simultaneously.

title: Full HYROX Simulation
desc: Full race simulation. Create conditions as close to race day as possible.

title: HYROX Strength Endurance
desc: HYROX-specific strength endurance. Heavy ascending compounds followed by machine intervals.

title: Deload — Row Sprints
desc: 30s max / 30s complete rest. Target 5000m total.

ex.note: Sprint as hard as possible. Target: 5000m.

title: Farmer Carry Intervals
desc: Race-specific farmer carry training with running intervals.

title: Sandbag Lunge Training
desc: Sandbag lunge practice. HYROX requires 100m — train for it.

title: Wall Ball Endurance
desc: Build the capacity to do 100 wall balls unbroken.

ex.note: Benchmark attempt.

title: Ski Erg Steady State
desc: Extended ski erg at conversational pace. Builds aerobic capacity specific to the ski movement.

ex.note: Zone 2, 60-70% HR max.

title: Burpee Broad Jump Intervals
desc: HYROX requires 80m of burpee broad jumps. Train for distance and pace under fatigue.

title: HYROX Weak Station Rotation
desc: Pick 2-3 weak HYROX stations. Do 5-6 targeted sets each with a 200m run between.

title: "Murph" — Hero WOD
desc: The most iconic Hero WOD. 1 mile + 100 pull-ups + 200 push-ups + 300 squats + 1 mile.

title: "DT" — Hero WOD
desc: 5 rounds for time: 12 deadlifts + 9 hang power cleans + 6 push jerks.

title: "Grace" — 30 Clean & Jerks
desc: 30 clean & jerks for time. Tests barbell cycling efficiency under fatigue.

title: "Isabel" — 30 Snatches
desc: 30 snatches for time. Tests snatch cycling ability at heavy-ish load.

title: "Angie" — 400 Reps For Time
desc: 100 each of pull-ups, push-ups, sit-ups, and air squats — in order, for time.

title: "Annie" — 50-40-30-20-10
desc: 50-40-30-20-10 reps of double-unders and sit-ups, for time.

title: "Diane" — 21-15-9
desc: 21-15-9 deadlifts and handstand push-ups.

title: "Jackie" — Row + Thrusters + Pull-ups
desc: 1000m row + 50 thrusters + 30 pull-ups, for time.

title: "Karen" — 150 Wall Balls
desc: 150 wall balls for time.

title: "Nancy" — 5 Rounds: Run + OHS
desc: 5 rounds of 400m run + 15 OHS @95/65 lb.

title: "Fight Gone Bad"
desc: 3 rounds, 5 stations, 1 min each, 1 min rest. Max total reps.

title: "King Kong" — 3 Rounds Extreme
desc: One of the hardest CrossFit benchmark WODs. Elite athletes only.

title: OWL Accessory Block
desc: Accessory work targeting the weak links in the Olympic lifts.

title: HYROX Engine Builder
desc: High-intensity 4-round circuit. Mimics the sustained effort of a HYROX race.

title: HYROX Half-Simulation
desc: Race pace 4-station half-simulation. Practice transitions.

title: HYROX Compromised Run
desc: 45-min AMRAP combining running with HYROX-specific stations.

title: 8×1km Running Intervals
desc: 8 rounds of 1km at race effort with equal rest.

ex.note: Race pace or slightly faster.

title: Session 11/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 4 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: spine, shoulder stability with rubber bands and birddog.

title: Session 12/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 4 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: ankle, knee, hip. Progresses to banded overhead squat.

title: Session 13/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 5 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: neck, wrist, chest progression. Incline behind-the-neck press.

title: Session 14/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 5 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: shoulder rotation, triceps band stretch.

title: Session 15/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 5 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC shoulder circles, arm abduction, sitting press progression.

title: Session 16/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 6 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Introduces PVC overhead duck walk and box front squat.

title: Session 17/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 6 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: hip, pigeon, hamstring, knee stability.

title: Session 18/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 6 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Continues PVC duck walk and box front squat.

title: Session 19/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 7 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: ankle, knee, squat patterns. Inside-band overhead squat.

ex.n: Supported leg kick with hip rotation

title: Session 20/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 7 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC frontal rotation, shoulder mobility, sitting press on 3 plates.

title: Session 21/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 7 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: hip, ankle, squat with Kosak shifts.

title: Session 22/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 8 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC circles, I-Y-T lead springs, halo.

title: Session 23/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 8 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: wrist, PVC shoulder, lat band, weighted deep squat.

title: Session 24/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 8 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Introduces Clean Sots Press.

title: Session 25/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 9 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Advanced rack hip muscle clean + front squat.

ex.n: PVC Rack Hip Muscle Clean + Front Squat

title: Session 26/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 9 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: calf, hip, inner thigh with plie variations. Plate overhead squat.

title: Session 27/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 9 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Introduces front squat with straps.

title: Session 28/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 10 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: hip, hamstring, knee. Plate overhead squat.

title: Session 29/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 10 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC upper body, arm abduction. Clean Sots Press progression.

title: Session 30/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 10 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Comprehensive 10-exercise lower body mobilization.

title: Session 31/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 11 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: spine, shoulder endurance with I-Y-T.

title: Session 32/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 11 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC rotators, shoulder band. Front squat with straps.

title: Session 33/36 — Overhead Mobility
desc: Week 11 · Session 3. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: PVC mobility, I-Y-T, halo.

title: Session 34/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 12 · Session 1. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Focus: spine, triceps band, Y-T barrier. Barbell tall clean.

title: Session 35/36 — Lower Body Mobility
desc: Week 12 · Session 2. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility. Final lower body session.

title: Session 36/36 — Front Rack Mobility
desc: Week 12 · Session 3 — FINAL SESSION. Torokhtiy Full-Body Mobility for Weightlifting. Complete the 12-week program.

title: 30-Min Max Distance Run Test
desc: Cycle 1 Test Week Day 1. Baseline 30-min max distance effort. Record how far you run — this number becomes your benchmark to beat each cycle.

ex.note: Build pace gradually.
Record total distance. Run on treadmill only if you plan to retest on treadmill. Negative split — no fast start.
Lower HR. Negative split this too — start easy, flush the legs.

title: HYROX Strength Test A — Deadlift + 5k
desc: Cycle 1 Test Week Day 3. Three-part strength test. 3-rep max deadlift, 100 wall balls for time, then a 5k ski or row test. Establishes your strength and endurance baselines.

ex.note: Be smart and safe. You are an athlete, not a powerlifter. Record your result.
Record time. Rest 5–8 min after before Part 3.
Prefer ski erg. Warm up 5–10 min easy before starting. Record time. Muscular endurance test — different from running.

title: HYROX Strength Test B — Bench + Pull-ups + Burpees
desc: Cycle 1 Test Week Day 5. Four-part test covering upper body strength, pull stamina, lunge endurance, and burpee speed. Record all scores.

ex.note: Record your result.
No kipping. No dropping from top. Chin over bar at top, full extension at bottom. Muscular stamina test — do not cheat reps.
Knee must touch ground, fully stand at top of each rep. Rest 5 min after.
Each rep: chest to floor, fully face bar, jump fully over bar to other side. 45 lb plate each side creates correct jump height.
Easy pace. Flush the legs.

title: Negative Split Run — 50 Min
desc: 50-min run with pace increasing every 10 minutes. Teaches pacing strategy critical for race day. Do not go hard early — last 20 min should feel like you are racing.

ex.note: Increase pace every 10 min. Min 0–10: easy (e.g. 9-min/mile). Min 11–20: moderate (8-min/mile). Continue stepping up. Last 10 min: above time-trial pace. After this run you should NOT be wrecked. Manage your effort level.

title: Strength Block A — Deadlift + Bench 3/2/1
desc: Hyrox-specific strength session. Heavy 3/2/1 loading on deadlift and bench. Accessory work with banded good mornings and DB push press. Keep this under an hour — it is not a conditioning session.

ex.note: Two rounds. After rep 1, re-rack and increase weight for round 2. 2–3 min rest between sets. Form over weight.
Same format. Increase weight 5 lb each week.
This session is about learning the skill of being strong. Keep it under an hour.

title: Sled Day + Lactate Buffer EMOM
desc: Cycle 1 Week 2/3 sled session. Heavy sled work followed by 16-min lactate buffer EMOM. Increases ability to work at high intensity while managing heart rate.

ex.note: Use race shoes and gloves. Heavier = better. Should be able to repeat the full length after rest.
Repeat entire cycle 4 times = 16 min total. Push pace each round.
Keep effort at 60% or less. Goal is mileage and flushing the legs.

title: Sled Day — Extended Sets + Alternate EMOM
desc: Cycle 1 Week 4 sled session. Longer sled distance (20m) and a double-under / lunge / burpee broad jump EMOM variant. More volume than Week 2/3.

ex.note: 20m sets are harder — manage weight so you complete each one unbroken.
Singles if you can't do double unders.
4 cycles = 16 min total. Find your limits on the last round.
60% or less. Flush the legs from earlier.

title: Zercher Lunges + Weighted Pull-ups + Row/Ski Intervals
desc: HYROX-specific ascending strength followed by high-volume 500m machine intervals. Builds leg strength, upper body pull capacity, and race-pace cardio endurance.

ex.note: Increase weight each round.
Use same machine as your time trial. Row 10–15s per 500m faster than your time trial pace.
On pumped legs. Easy pace.

title: 1-Mile Time Trial — De-load Check
desc: Cycle 1 Week 5 de-load. Low-stakes 1-mile time trial to check running progress. Not a max effort — a fun benchmark to see how the training is paying off.

ex.note: Increase pace slowly over 10 minutes.
This is here for fun and as a progress check-up. Do not kill yourself. Record time.

title: De-load Strength + Metcon
desc: Cycle 1 Week 5 de-load strength day. Heavy singles on deadlift and bench, upper body gymnastics, then a three-movement metcon for time.

ex.note: Get after it :)

title: 30s/30s Row Sprints — 5000m
desc: Cycle 1 De-load Day 4. Sprint rowing with complete stops during rest. Target 5000m total. Elite scores: Male ≤26 rounds, Female ≤30 min.

ex.note: Sprint as hard as possible. STOP completely during rest — do not paddle. Target: 5000m total. Elite male: 26 rounds or less. Elite female: 30 min or less.

title: Sled + Conditioning WOD
desc: Cycle 2 Week 1 sled day. Sled sets with 400m runs between, then a heavy for-time conditioning WOD: 2000m row + 100 wall balls + 50 push-ups + 25 deadlifts at race weight.

ex.note: Run at recovery pace. Practice running on pumped sled legs.
Keep HR and perceived effort around 80%. Stay in control — you should be able to push harder but choose not to.
60% or less. Flush the legs.

title: Hang Power Clean + Bench + Monster Sets
desc: Cycle 2 Week 1 strength session. Introduces hang power clean and paused bench. Monster sets build muscular endurance in push, pull, and KB movement patterns.

ex.note: Two rounds. Increase weight round 2. 2–3 min rest. Use your rest — needed for heavy movement.
Pause at chest before pressing. Two rounds, increase weight. Increase 5 lb from last week.
3 rounds minimum, 4 if rep count stays high and form stays strong. Not meant to be long or sweaty — learn the skill of being strong.

title: HYROX Easy Training Run
desc: Standard Day 2 / Day 4 recovery run in the HYROX training program. 50–55 min easy or 75 min bike if your body is trashed from yesterday. Keep below 70% effort — this is about moving without compromising tomorrow's session.

ex.note: Keep effort below 70% HR max. Alternatively: 75 min easy bike. Today is about moving and flushing the legs, not getting in the way of tomorrow's effort. Even if you feel good, hold back.

title: HYROX Long Run — Zone 2
desc: Weekly long aerobic run that builds the endurance needed for race day. 80–90 min at below 70% effort. Do not force pace — it may take time to adjust to this training style. This is a key weekly session.

ex.note: Adjust based on fitness. Keep RPE and heart rate below 70% effort level. Do not force yourself to run faster if your body isn't ready. This session becomes a pillar of your race-day preparation.

title: HYROX Short Recovery Run
desc: PM recovery run after a heavy sled or strength session. 35–40 min at 60% effort or less. Goal is mileage and flushing the legs — not fitness.

ex.note: Keep effort at 60% or less. Goal: accumulate easy miles and flush out the legs from the earlier heavy session. Do not let ego push the pace here.

title: Week 4 S&C — Zercher + Pull-ups + 12×500m
desc: Cycle 1 Week 4 strength and conditioning. Same ascending Zercher + pull-up strength structure as weeks 2–3, but volume increases to 12×500m machine intervals. The extra set is your Week 4 progression.

ex.note: Increase weight each round.
Increase weight each round.
Use same machine as time trial. 10–15s per 500m faster than your time trial pace. One more set than previous weeks.
On pumped legs. Easy pace.

title: De-load S&C — Week 5 Day 5
desc: Cycle 1 Week 5 de-load strength and conditioning. Lighter set/rep scheme on Zercher lunges and pull-ups, then a punchy 21/15/9 conditioning piece. Get this done quickly and move on.

ex.note: Increase weight each round. Lighter volume than main training weeks.
Make the weight heavy. Get this done quickly then move on.
Push the pace hard here — it will all be over soon.
Goal is to complete movements unbroken each round.

title: Cycle 2 S&C — Bulgarian Split Squat + Chin-ups + 5×1000m
desc: Cycle 2 Week 1 Day 5. New strength movement pairing: DB Bulgarian split squat replaces Zercher lunge. 5×1000m intervals are longer than the 500m sets from Cycle 1 — a step up in conditioning demand.

ex.note: Increase weight each round.
Increase weight each round.
Use same machine as time trial. 10–15s faster than time trial pace. These larger reps challenge pacing — you are in shape for the load, now learn to control yourself throughout.
On pumped legs. Easy pace.

title: Cycle 2 Long Run — Last Mile Sprint
desc: Cycle 2 Week 1 Day 6. 80-min long run at sub-70% effort, finishing with one final mile at max effort. Simulates the race-day experience of having a strong kick at the end after a long sustained effort.

ex.note: Keep RPE and heart rate below 70%. Do not force pace. Adjusted based on your fitness level.
After 70 min at e.g. 8-min/mile, your goal is to hit a 6-min/mile on the last mile. Push hard — this is your race-day kick.

title: Test Week Easy Recovery
desc: Test Week Days 2 and 4. Easy active recovery between high-intensity testing days. 30 min easy run or 60 min hike. Make the choice based on how hard yesterday felt.

ex.note: Make the choice based off how you feel from yesterday's hard effort. Some of you will recover harder from speed work — stay healthy and strong during training. Bike is also great if you need more recovery time.

title: Test Week Long Aerobic — Day 6
desc: Cycle 1 Test Week Day 6. Long easy aerobic effort after a heavy test week. 90-min hike or bike at below 70% effort. Keeps intensity very low so you are fully recovered for next week's training.

ex.note: Keep your RPE or heart rate below 70% effort level. Since we had a heavy test week we want to keep the intensity very low to make sure you are fully recovered for next week's training. This will become a key session each week for building the endurance needed for race day.

title: HYROX Compromised Run — Sled + Station AMRAP
desc: 45-min AMRAP that combines running with sled and wall balls. Simulates the accumulated fatigue of a HYROX race where each station compromises your running ability. Score: total rounds.

ex.note: Heavy but controllable.
Practise running on pumped sled legs.
Consistent sets — don't go unbroken and blow up.
